folio.label: 92r
folio.l: And brynge me æa~neæa~ne twelue freres , wete ©e why 550The correction is done with the same ink as the one on F91v. In dark brown ink.ODR
For threttene , is a couent , as I gesse
ñoure confessoure heere , for his worthynesse
Shal ¹fourme vp , the noumbre ofthisof thisof,this Couent553of this: A vertical space marker separates these two words.JRT
Than shuln~ they knele a doun , by oon assent
And to euäy spokes ende , in this manere
Ful sadly leyn his nose , shal a frere
ñoure noble confessour , there god hi~ saue
Shal holde his nose vp rigút , vnder the naue
Than shal this cherl , witú bely stif and tougút
As any tabour , hedir ben I brougút
And sette hi~ on the whel , rigút of this cart
Vp on the naue , and make hi~ late a fart
And ©e shuln sen , vp periÂ of my lif
By preeue , whicú that is , demonstratif´
That equally , the sovne of it , wyl wende
Ad eke the stynk , vn to the spokes ende
Saue that this worthy man , ©oure confessour
Bycause he is a man , of gret honour
Shal han the first fruyt , as resou~ is
The noble vsage of freres , ©et is this
The worthy men of hem , shuln first be serued
And certeynly , he hatú it wel deserued
He hatú to day taugút vs , so mechil good
Witú prechynge in the pulpyt , there he stod
That I may vouche saf , I seye for me
He had the first smel , of fartes three
And so wold al his Couent , hardily
He beretú hi~ so fayre , and so holily
¢ The lord the lady , and eche man saue the frere
Seyden that Iankyn , spak in this matere
As wel as Euclide , or protholome
Towchynge the cherles , they seyden sotiltee
An AnxxxyAn heyAnxxxheywyt , made hi~ speke , as he spak
He nys no fool , ne noo demonyak
And Iankyn hatú I wonne , a newe Govne
My Tale is doon , we ben almost at Tovne

note.notetxt: 586 In the right margin next to this line appears: ¢ q¶ Wytton~JRT

folio.l: ¢ Hic finitå fabula Su~monitoris , & incipit ¸logus c&lbar;ici de Oxonö

note.notetxt: 586/1 Approximately eight lines of blank space between L 585 and explicit. The incipit is in the same ink of the tale of the Clerk - odd that it is at the bottom of the page and not after the end of SU. Thsi si a clue on the progress of copying of the manuscript.ODR
